steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS W/O CONTRAST)

discordance.evidence: Lactate: 9.5 mmol/L ... BP 107/55 / RR35 / spO2 94% ... HR 147 ... Troponin T: 0.06 ng/mL (ref 0.0-0.01) ... Potassium: 7.5 mEq/L ... Sodium: 120.0 mEq/L ... Anion Gap: 28.0 mEq/L ... Creatinine: 2.1 mg/dL ... Bicarbonate: 18.0 mEq/L ... Lipase: 12.0 IU/L (ref 0.0-60.0)

alarm_detect.summary: Extreme hemodynamic instability (HR 147, BP 107/55, RR 35, lactate 9.5 mmol/L, anion gap 28, hyperkalemia 7.5, hyponatremia 120, elevated troponin) signals impending multi-organ failure and a life-threatening systemic process — far out of proportion to any single abdominal diagnosis such as cholecystitis or appendicitis — demanding immediate resuscitation and broad reassessment before any routine next step.

alarm_resolve.reason: The CT abdomen can identify an intra-abdominal source but the reasoning focuses narrowly on localizing abdominal pathology without acknowledging or planning to address the extreme systemic derangements (lactate 9.5, hyperkalemia 7.5, hyponatremia 120, elevated troponin, hemodynamic instability) that signal multi-organ failure far exceeding any single abdominal diagnosis — leaving the broader life-threatening systemic crisis unaddressed.
